title: Whisk & Bloom
description: Whisk & Bloom is where I treated storytelling and visual craft as core engineering outcomes, not cosmetic add-ons.

content.text: This project started from a different question: how do you preserve food stories with care, not just publish recipes quickly?
I could have built a dynamic app with accounts and comments, but the mission needed fast, elegant, readable pages more than feature complexity.
So I chose a static architecture and focused deeply on typography, responsive imagery, and content rhythm to make each recipe feel like a cultural story.
Saying no to unnecessary features made the core experience stronger.
Image behavior across devices needed iteration. I introduced responsive sources and loading strategy so mobile stayed fast while desktop stayed rich.
For this project, design quality was engineering quality.
I kept resisting feature creep and prioritized consistency across pages. That decision made the site feel curated instead of stitched together.
Whisk & Bloom reinforced a core lesson: constraints are powerful when they are chosen intentionally and executed with discipline.

content.suggestion: Smooth transition from recipe card preview to full story-rich recipe layout across mobile and desktop
